user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the rear of a silver car with extensive crush damage and a missing/dislodged rear bumper, plus deformation of the trunk and rear body panels.
claim_status_justification: The rear bumper is severely damaged and detached, with major deformation extending into the trunk lid and rear body, consistent with the claimed rear-end damage but more extensive than a simple dent.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The two images show different vehicles. img_1 is a white compact sedan with significant front bumper damage including dents, scuffs and deformation near the headlight. img_2 is a white Jaguar luxury sedan with an intact, undamaged front bumper.
claim_status_justification: The claim describes a scratch on the front bumper. img_1 shows damage but it is more than a light scratch — there is denting and material deformation around the bumper and headlight area (medium severity), which exceeds the 'light scratch' described. img_2 shows a completely different, undamaged car. Because the images depict two distinct vehicles, the set is not cross-image consistent, and the claimed light bumper scratch is not consistently supported.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 shows a windshield with extensive shattering/spider-web cracking radiating from an impact point, consistent with the claimed stone strike and spreading crack. img_2 shows an intact, undamaged windshield of a different colored vehicle and does not depict damage.
claim_status_justification: The claimed windshield damage is clearly visible in img_1, showing a large impact with extensive radiating cracks. img_2 shows an intact windshield on what appears to be a different vehicle, so the set is not fully consistent, but img_1 alone provides sufficient evidence to evaluate the claim.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the red car's side mirror with a cracked/shattered mirror glass, consistent with the claimed damage.
claim_status_justification: The side mirror is visible at a clear angle and shows visible cracking/breakage of the mirror surface, supporting the claim of side mirror damage. Damage is localized, so severity is medium.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_2 clearly shows the rear bumper area with an added rear bar but no visible damage; img_1 shows a dent on a front fender/body area, not the rear bumper, and appears to be a different part/vehicle.
claim_status_justification: The rear bumper in img_2 is visible from a clear angle and shows no dents, cracks, or deformation. img_1 shows a dent but it is on a side/front fender body panel, not the claimed rear bumper, and the two images appear to depict different vehicles. The part is assessable and the claimed rear-bumper damage is not present.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image shows a black car on a road at sunset, but the front headlight is not clearly visible due to the rear-side angle and heavy glare; the headlight cannot be inspected for cracks.
claim_status_justification: Only the rear/side of the vehicle is shown with strong backlight glare. The claimed headlight is not visible at an angle that allows assessment of cracking, so the claim cannot be evaluated from this image.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 is too blurry to assess and shows a red/black hatchback, while img_2 is a sharp close-up of a black car's lower door/rocker panel showing a visible dent/deformation. The two images appear to show different vehicles, but img_2 clearly depicts the claimed door-area dent.
claim_status_justification: img_1: heavily blurred side view of a red and black car; door area cannot be inspected for deformation due to blur. img_2: clear, well-lit close-up of a black vehicle's lower door panel and rocker, showing a distinct dent/crease with disturbed reflections, sufficient to confirm a localized dent in the door area. The vehicles differ in color, so cross-image consistency fails, but img_2 alone supports the claimed door dent.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image shows a car with the hood open and severe front-end collision damage exposing the engine bay, not a scratch on an intact hood top panel. The hood surface cannot be assessed for the claimed scratch, and the image bears 'Vecteezy' stock watermarks indicating it is not an original customer photo.
claim_status_justification: The claim concerns a cosmetic scratch across the top of the hood. The submitted image instead depicts catastrophic front-end crash damage with the hood raised, so the claimed scratch on the hood surface cannot be evaluated. Multiple 'Vecteezy' watermarks indicate a stock image rather than an original photo, undermining its evidentiary value.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The open laptop clearly shows the display screen with extensive spider-web cracking radiating from a central impact point, consistent with a fall.
claim_status_justification: The claimed screen is fully visible and shows severe shattered glass damage, matching the reported crack from the fall. Damage is extensive across the entire panel.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows a damaged/broken hinge area with the bezel separated and internal components exposed, consistent with the broken hinge claim. img_2 shows an intact, pristine laptop that appears to be a different device (likely a stock-style image), inconsistent with img_1.
claim_status_justification: The close-up in img_1 displays the laptop's hinge/corner region with the screen bezel detached and broken parts visible, supporting the claim of a broken hinge. img_2 depicts a fully intact laptop on a wooden table with no visible hinge damage and looks like a generic studio shot, making it inconsistent with img_1. At least one relevant image (img_1) shows the claimed part well enough to evaluate, so evidence is sufficient.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the laptop keyboard area covered with visible water droplets and pooling liquid across the keys, consistent with the claimed water spill.
claim_status_justification: The keyboard is clearly visible with abundant water present on and around the keys, matching the claimed water exposure to the keyboard area.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 shows the intact silver Dell laptop lid for context, and img_2 is a close-up of a corner area showing a localized dent/impact mark on the metal surface near the corner.
claim_status_justification: The close-up image (img_2) clearly shows a corner of a silver laptop with a visible dent/impact deformation, consistent with the claim of a corner dent from a fall. img_1 provides overall context confirming the laptop. The damage is localized, so severity is medium.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The MacBook Pro screen is clearly visible and shows a spider-web crack pattern radiating from a central impact point, consistent with the claimed screen damage.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows an open MacBook Pro with a distinct shattered/cracked display radiating from an impact point center-right, with secondary cracks across the panel. This matches the customer's claim of screen damage. The part is clearly assessable.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the laptop trackpad area with a hand-drawn circle marking, but the surface appears smooth and intact with no visible cracks, dents, or physical damage.
claim_status_justification: The trackpad and surrounding palm rest are visible in good lighting and clearly enough to inspect for physical damage. No physical damage is apparent in the circled area, so the visible evidence does not support the claim of physical damage around the trackpad.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the top corner of a cardboard box that is crushed and dented inward, consistent with the claimed corner damage.
claim_status_justification: The package corner is visible and shows crushing/denting at the top edge, matching the claim of a crushed corner. The 'TEAM LIFT' and 'CAUTION HEAVY PACKAGE' text is ordinary shipping labeling, not reviewer-directed instruction. No manipulation indicators present.

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows a cardboard box with the top/seal side torn open along the taped seam, with cardboard ripped and edges peeling, consistent with the torn packaging claim. img_2 shows an intact, undamaged box that appears to be a different physical object.
claim_status_justification: The torn seal-side packaging claim is supported by img_1, which shows significant tearing along the taped seam. img_2 shows an intact box and is inconsistent with img_1, indicating they may not be the same object, so cross_image_consistent is false; however img_1 alone provides sufficient evidence of the localized torn packaging.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows a cardboard box with extensive dark wet-looking staining across the side surface, along with softened and torn cardboard consistent with water damage.
claim_status_justification: The package exterior side is visible and shows a large dark stain with discoloration and softened/deformed cardboard, matching the claimed water damage to the package surface.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 shows an open box filled with crumpled paper/packaging material rather than an empty interior, and no ordered product is identifiable. img_2 shows a closed, sealed box exterior with a barcode label and does not show contents. The two images appear to be different boxes.
claim_status_justification: The open box in img_1 contains packing filler, not a clearly empty cavity, and the expected item cannot be identified, so missing contents cannot be confirmed. img_2 only shows a sealed exterior. The images appear to depict different packages, so cross-image consistency fails and the claim of missing contents cannot be adequately assessed.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image shows a metal food can with a Nutrition Facts label, not a shipping box. No package exterior or crushed box is visible.
claim_status_justification: The claim concerns a crushed shipping box, but the submitted image depicts a canned food product on a shelf. The relevant object (shipping box) is not shown, so the crushed-box damage cannot be assessed.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows a box with the tamper-evident seal torn open at the corner, consistent with the claim. img_2 shows a different, intact box with security seal tape and appears to be a stock photo (watermark), inconsistent with img_1.
claim_status_justification: img_1 displays a tamper-evident sealed box with the seal/tape visibly torn and the corner opened, supporting the torn-seal claim. However img_2 depicts a separate, undamaged box with intact security seals and bears a stock-photo watermark, so the two images do not show the same physical object.
